# Release Notes: Hypercolor 0.2.0

**Released:** 2026-07-14

Hypercolor 0.2.0 is the first public release: an open-source, Linux-first RGB lighting engine built from scratch in Rust. 25 workspace crates, 15 hardware driver families, 45 SDK effects, 11 native builtin renderers, 7 display faces, a Leptos web UI, a Ratatui TUI, a Tauri desktop app, a Python client, and a TypeScript SDK, all shipping in a single coordinated release.

## ✨ Highlights

### Sparkleflinger Render Pipeline
The daemon runs a dedicated render thread called **Sparkleflinger** that composes effect layers onto a 640x480 RGBA canvas every frame. It supports CPU and GPU (wgpu) compositor backends, with GPU acceleration auto-detected at startup. Spatial sampling maps the composed canvas to physical LED positions using bilinear or Gaussian area sampling. Scene transitions crossfade between effect changes. Frame admission control, surface pooling, and deferred GPU readbacks keep per-frame allocations near zero.

### Multi-Zone Scene Engine
Scenes organize devices into named **zones**, each running its own effect layer stack independently. Zones can target different effects, presets, and control values simultaneously. The default scene auto-creates a "Default" zone so single-zone setups work without configuration. Multi-zone previews stream per-zone frames over WebSocket. The layer stack supports effect, media, screen-region, and web-viewport source types with per-layer blend mode, opacity, and transform controls.

### 15 Hardware Driver Families
Native USB, HID, SMBus, serial, and network drivers ship for: **Razer** (keyboards, mice, laptops, the Seiren V3 Chroma, scroll wheel LEDs), **Corsair** (Lighting Node, iCUE LINK, LCD coolers, Bragi peripherals), **ASUS Aura** (USB and SMBus DRAM), **Lian Li** (UNI HUB AL/SL/TL, ENE, and legacy controllers), **Nollie/PrismRGB** (Gen1, Gen2, NOS2, Legacy, Stream65), **Dygma** (Defy split keyboard via Focus protocol), **QMK** (OpenRGB HID protocol), **Ableton Push 2** (MIDI LEDs and LCD display), **ROLI Blocks** (via blocksd bridge), **WLED** (DDP/E1.31 over UDP), **Philips Hue** (Entertainment API streaming with DTLS), **Nanoleaf** (panel topology and UDP streaming), **Govee** (LAN multicast and cloud API with rate limiting), and **OpenRGB** (SDK TCP bridge as a fallback for unsupported devices). Each driver uses zerocopy typed structs for protocol encoding.

### TypeScript Effect SDK
The `@hypercolor/sdk` npm package provides a declarative `effect()` and `canvas()` API for authoring LED effects and a `face()` API for display faces. Effects can use 2D Canvas, WebGL shaders (GLSL), or raw HTML. The SDK ships with a `hypercolor` CLI (`build`, `validate`, `install`, `dev`, `create`) and a `create-hypercolor` scaffolder. 45 effects ship in the SDK spanning ambient, audio-reactive, generative, and interactive categories. The build pipeline bundles each effect into a self-contained HTML file with inlined assets and validates metadata at build time.

### Desktop Application
`hypercolor-app` wraps the Leptos web UI in a Tauri shell with a managed daemon supervisor. It spawns and monitors the daemon process, reconnects on crashes with exponential backoff and a circuit breaker, logs to rolling files, and integrates with the system tray (brightness presets, play/pause, diagnostics export). On Windows, an NSIS per-machine installer bundles the SMBus broker service, PawnIO kernel modules for CPU temperature readings, and ANGLE EGL DLLs for Servo GPU import. On macOS, a hardened `.app` bundle is built with `just mac-build`. On Linux, the app prefers systemd user services for daemon management.

### GPU Surface Interop
Servo (the embedded browser engine rendering HTML/JS effects) can hand off rendered frames to the Sparkleflinger GPU compositor without CPU readback. On Linux, this uses Vulkan external memory export into OpenGL via `EXT_external_objects_fd` with fence synchronization. On macOS, IOSurface blit with GL fences provides the same zero-copy path. On Windows, ANGLE shared D3D11 textures are imported through a fence ring. When GPU import fails, the system falls back to CPU readback automatically.

## 🤖 Core Engine

- `hypercolor-types` defines the shared type system: devices, effects, scenes, zones, layers, controls, spatial layouts, sensors, media assets, and all API DTOs
- `hypercolor-core` implements the effect registry, Servo web engine integration, LightScript audio bridge, spatial sampler, device backends (USB, SMBus, ROLI Blocks), event bus, scene manager, session/power awareness, screen capture (Wayland portal), sensor input, and MPRIS now-playing
- `hypercolor-daemon` runs the render thread, REST/WebSocket API (Axum), MCP server (Model Context Protocol for AI agents), device discovery and lifecycle management, display face output workers, and profile/preset persistence
- `hypercolor-hal` contains the hardware abstraction layer with protocol implementations for all USB/HID/serial/SMBus device families, plus a vendor device database (`data/drivers/vendors/*.toml`)

## 🎨 Effects and Faces

45 SDK effects ship across categories: **Ambient** (Solid Color, Gradient, Rainbow, Breathing, Color Wave, Color Zones, Borealis, Ember Glow, Nebula Drift, Hyperspace, Ink Tide, Vortex), **Generative** (Arc Storm, Breakthrough, Bubble Garden, Cyber Descent, Deep Current, Digital Rain, Fiberflies, Fractalux, Frost Crystal, Hyperbloom, Lava Lamp, Meteor Storm, Neon City, Nyan Dash, Plasma Engine, Poisonous, Roller Rink, Spectral Fire, Starfall, Swirl Reactor, Synth Horizon, Voidweaver, Voronoi Glass, Wormhole), **Audio-reactive** (Audio Pulse, Cymatics, Frequency Cascade, Iris, ADHD Hyperfocus, Shockwave), **Utility** (Calibration, Screen Cast, Ambilight), and **Source** (Color Zones, Gradient with Oklch). 11 native Rust builtin renderers cover the always-available utility layer (solid color, gradient, rainbow, breathing, audio pulse, color wave, color zones, screen cast, media player, calibration, web viewport).

7 display faces for LCD-equipped devices (Corsair LCD coolers, Ableton Push 2): **Neon Clock** (digit morphing, sweep seconds), **Pulse Temp** (banded history with threshold pulse), **Sensor Grid** (staggered layout cells with sparklines), **SilkCircuit HUD** (layered background with boot choreography), **Now Playing** (album art and track progress), **Spectrum** (liquid spectral terrain), and **System Pulse** (multi-metric cards with net panel). Faces use a shared atmosphere kit for nebula backgrounds and are built with vendored fonts for hermetic rendering.

## 🔧 CLI and TUI

- `hypercolor` CLI: connection profiles (`hypercolor servers adopt`), effect/preset/scene/zone/driver/control management, `hyper status` with rich visual output, SilkCircuit-themed help with gradient brand banner, shell completions, `just dev` workflow, `hypercolor tui` launches the terminal UI as a subcommand
- `hypercolor-tui`: Ratatui-based terminal interface with 60fps rendering, live canvas preview (Kitty/Sixel/halfblock protocols), spectrum-reactive border pulse, idle breathing animation, HSL color picker, mouse-draggable sliders, resizable split panels, multi-zone and multi-scene support, auto-start local daemon when none is reachable

## 🌐 Web UI (Leptos/WASM)

- **Dashboard** with drag-to-reorder panels, live performance telemetry (frame timeline waterfall, pipeline breakdown, surface pool gauges), favorites panel, and cinematic preview cabinet
- **Effects** page with 60fps WebGL preview, live-captured thumbnails, preset dropdown, zone-tabbed controls, and per-zone now-playing
- **Studio** page: multi-zone composition workspace with zone tree, device assignment, per-zone layer stacks, per-zone preview, layout editor with undo/redo, and display face management
- **Devices** page with brand-accent device cards, per-device metrics charts, driver control surfaces, and pairing modals
- **Displays** page for LCD display face management with blend modes, live JPEG preview streaming, and simulator creation
- **Settings** with sectioned navigation, live audio VU meter, driver inventory, and session/power controls
- **Luminary** design system: SilkCircuit-themed tokens, light/dark mode, ambient hue extraction from the running effect, animated glows instead of shadows

## 🐍 Python Client

The `hypercolor` PyPI package provides async and sync clients generated from the daemon's OpenAPI spec. WebSocket streaming, effect uploads, scene/zone management, and driver control surfaces are supported. Home Assistant integration helpers are included.

## ⚡ Performance

- Zero-copy surface publication with `Arc`-backed canvases and generation tracking
- GPU compositor (wgpu) with cached bind groups, dynamic-offset buffer rings, and fused compose-and-sample submission
- Deferred GPU zone sampling overlaps readback waits with output push work
- Lock-free WebSocket relay subscription snapshots, sharded payload caches, and event-driven preview relays
- Linear-light color pipeline with sRGB encode/decode LUTs for perceptual compensation
- Frame admission hysteresis for stable FPS tier selection under load
- Per-device output actors with async transport acknowledgements and EWMA delivery rate tracking
- Criterion benchmark suites for the core pipeline, HAL encoding, and GPU compositor

## 🔒 Security

- API auth tiers with configurable network access modes (loopback-only, LAN, or open)
- CORS policy bound to auth configuration
- Origin validation on WebSocket upgrades
- Credential file mode restrictions (0600)
- Web viewport URLs restricted to public HTTP/HTTPS
- Stream URL SSRF validation with IPv6 bypass protection
- Media admission caps per scene
- Bounded animated media decode to prevent OOM
- PawnIO payload verification on Windows
- Cross-site request forgery blocking for loopback writes

## 📦 Packaging and Distribution

- `scripts/dist.sh` produces release tarballs with daemon, CLI, effects, faces, udev rules, systemd units, and desktop files
- AUR PKGBUILD and Homebrew formula for CLI and desktop app cask
- NSIS Windows installer with per-machine install, SMBus broker service, and PawnIO module deployment
- macOS `.app` bundle with hardened runtime entitlements
- Debian package generation via `scripts/package-deb.sh`
- CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Rust build caching, Servo cache warming, Playwright e2e tests, release workflow with artifact rehearsal mode, npm trusted publishing for the SDK

## 🔌 API Surface

- REST API with OpenAPI spec served at `/api/openapi.json`
- WebSocket protocol with typed binary channels for canvas frames, spectrum data, device metrics, and per-zone previews
- MCP server (Model Context Protocol) for AI agent integration: effect/scene/device/display tools, prompts, and resources served over both stdio and HTTP
- Extension seam (`hypercolor-daemon/src/extensions.rs`) for internal features behind the OSS boundary

## Upgrade Notes

This is the initial public release. There is no prior version to upgrade from.

- Install via `just setup` (Linux/macOS) or `scripts/setup.ps1` (Windows) to bootstrap all build dependencies
- Run `just dev` to start the daemon with the web UI in development mode
- Effects are built with `just effects` and require Bun
- The `hypercolor` CLI binary and `hypercolor-daemon` binary are the primary distribution artifacts
- MSRV is Rust 1.94 (workspace) / 1.95 (for Servo features)
- The Servo HTML effect renderer requires a prebuilt servo cache or source build; see `docs/development/SERVO_BUILD_CACHING.md`
